description: Clusters of small capsules start out green but turn yellow and split to reveal a scarlet, fleshy, berry-like fruit that has three sections each with 2 white seeds, mature between August and January.

care.soil_types: Soil with loose texture or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions, apply 1-2 times monthly
care.watering_guide: Missouri BG: Easily grown in most soils. Best in lean to average soils with regular moisture in full sun. Lean soils help restrain growth. care.tips: Remove aged tissues, dead and pest branches timely | Similar plants: Vitex rotundifolia | Native to: VRG OO, CHN SA, BUL OO, ASIA TROPICAL, NORTHERN EUROPE
common_problems: No serious insect or disease problems

Chinese bittersweet should not be planted in the eastern or midwestern U
Where appropriate, it can be an interesting addition to woodland gardens and naturalized areas
Provides quick cover for fences, arbors, trellises, posts, walls or other structures in the landscape

description: The fruit is round 1/2  diameter, dry three lobed capsule that starts out green turning the outside orange which eventuallly splits exposing red seeds.  They appear in fall and persist through winter.

description: Small 1/2 inch long, red, fleshy fruit ripens in fall within a red capsule. This red, pink, ivory to yellow capsule splits to show orange-red seeds in the fall that are attractive to birds. The fall berries are eaten by birds, and the seeds are easily spread by birds. An obovoid, dehiscent capsule; single seed enclosed in orange-red aril.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: 3/4 inch red bumpy 5-lobed capsule splits to reveal red seeds. Displays from September to October.

care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight)
common_problems: No serious problems, aside from browsing by deer.
common_pests: Scale insects can be a problem.

description: The flowers are replaced by 4-lobed seed capsules that become mature during the fall. Each seed capsule splits open into four parts to expose fleshy red fruits) that each contain two seeds. The seed capsules are light pink or pale purple with a smooth surface; they later become more dark-colored. The seeds are light brown with a smooth surface.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Euonymus europaeus, the spindle, European spindle, or common spindle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to much of Europe, where it inhabits the edges of forest, hedges and gentle slopes, tending to thrive on nutrient-rich, chalky and salt-poor soils. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Ampelopsis glandulosa var. brevipedunculata

description: Fertile flowers are replaced by seed capsules that become mature during the autumn. Mature seed capsules are white, globoid, and about ¼ inch across. Eventually, they split open to reveal fleshy arils that are orange to red; solitary seeds occurs within these arils.

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)

description: Fruit is a reddish, pink, ivory to yellow capsule, splitting and showing orange-red seeds. The 4-valved capsule is globose, pink, 1/3 of an inch long with white seeds.

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
common_problems: Powdery mildew can be a problem, as can mites, leaf miners, aphids, mealybugs, and crown gall.
Additional diseases which may appear include witches  broom, stem dieback, and fungal leaf spots.
It can also become weedy in disturbed areas around houses and buildings.
common_pests: It is susceptible to euonymus scale and is frequently damaged by deer.
Powdery mildew can be a problem, as can mites, leaf miners, aphids, mealybugs, and crown gall.
Plants can be susceptible to attacks by caterpillars and are often damaged during the flowering season.

description: The capsule holding the berry is pink and seeds are 4 lobed and bright red.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_pests: Euonymus scale is a problem with most species of this plant.

description: Parnassia palustris, the marsh grass of Parnassus, northern grass-of-Parnassus, or just grass-of-Parnassus, and bog star, is a flowering plant in the staff-vine family Celastraceae.
